California Gold Nutrition, High Potency Vitamin B Complex with Methyl Folate & Methyl B12, 90 Veggie Capsules

Notice: Your cart contains melatonin

The item contains Melatonin and is subject to import restrictions in Australia, regulated by the Therapeutic Goods Administration (TGA). You may be asked by Australian authorities to provide proof of a valid prescription upon import. If you do not have a valid prescription, your order could be stopped at customs. 
By proceeding, you acknowledge that it is your responsibility to ensure compliance with local requirements. For additional information, please see regulation of melatonin products in Australia.

    AI overview

    AI generated. Not medical advice.

    What it's for

    Energy metabolism support; Nervous system support; Methylation support

    Who it may fit

    Adults with busy schedules seeking daily B vitamin intake or people with low intake of B-rich foods.

    Key actives per serving

    • Niacin (nicotinic acid and niacinamide): 140 mg (875% DV)
    • Pantothenic Acid (as d-calcium pantothenate): 110 mg (2200% DV)
    • Thiamin (as thiamine HCl): 110 mg (9167% DV)
    • Folate (as (6S)-5-methyltetrahydrofolic acid, glucosamine salt): 670 mcg DFE (168% DV)
    • Vitamin B12 (as methylcobalamin): 400 mcg (16667% DV)

    How to take

    Take 1 capsule once daily with food; taking with a meal can improve tolerance of niacin and other B vitamins.

    Diet / Allergen fit

    Vegan and Vegetarian; Formulated without Gluten, GMOs, or Soy; Not manufactured with milk, eggs, fish, crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soy, sesame, or gluten; Produced in a facility that may process other allergens.

    Ingredients explained

    Niacin (nicotinic acid and niacinamide)

    Niacin is converted to the coenzymes NAD and NADP, which are central to cellular energy metabolism and redox reactions.

    Caution: High amounts may cause flushing or stomach discomfort in some people; take with food to reduce irritation.

    Pantothenic Acid (as d-calcium pantothenate)

    Pantothenic acid forms part of coenzyme A, which is required for fatty acid metabolism and energy production.

    Caution: Very high doses may cause mild gastrointestinal upset; take with food if sensitive.

    Thiamin (as thiamine HCl)

    Thiamin is used to form thiamin pyrophosphate, a coenzyme that supports carbohydrate metabolism and nerve function.

    Caution: Excess thiamin is usually excreted, but very high doses may cause mild digestive discomfort in sensitive individuals.

    Folate (as (6S)-5-methyltetrahydrofolic acid, glucosamine salt)

    5-MTHF is the active folate form that donates methyl groups for homocysteine remethylation and supports DNA synthesis.

    Caution: Consult a healthcare professional if pregnant, breastfeeding, or using medications that affect folate metabolism because dosing needs can vary.

    Vitamin B12 (as methylcobalamin)

    Methylcobalamin supports methylation reactions and red blood cell formation by acting as a cofactor in homocysteine metabolism.

    Caution: People with B12-sensitive acne may notice breakouts at high intakes; separate from acidic beverages if stomach sensitivity occurs.

    How they work together

    Core B vitamins act as coenzymes in energy pathways: niacin and pantothenic acid drive redox and acetyl-CoA reactions, while thiamin supports carbohydrate metabolism. Methylated folate and methylcobalamin provide ready-to-use forms for methylation processes and homocysteine balance.

    Safety and tips

    Label Warning: Consult a licensed healthcare professional before using this dietary supplement.

    Who should be cautious: People sensitive to high-dose niacin may experience flushing; consider taking with food. Those with kidney concerns may wish to monitor total vitamin B6 intake from all sources. If pregnant, nursing, have a medical condition, or take medications, discuss use with a healthcare provider before use.

    Storage: Store in a cool, dry place. Protect from heat, light, and moisture.

    What customers say

    4.5
    Based on 7,606 ratings

    Noticeable energy support

    Many users mention feeling more energized after taking the B complex.

    Easy to swallow

    Capsules are commonly described as simple to take with no difficulty.

    Potential niacin flush or sensitivity

    A few users report flushing or allergic-type reactions likely related to the high-potency formula.

    Product information

    Overview

    • Supports Energy Production and Healthy Nervous System*
    • Suitable for Vegetarians and Vegans 
    • Formulated without Gluten, GMOs, or Soy


    California Gold Nutrition® High Potency Vitamin B Complex contains all 8 B vitamins — B1 (thiamine), B2, (riboflavin), B3 (niacin), B5 (pantothenic acid), B6 (pyridoxine), B7 (biotin), B9 (folate), and B12 (cobalamin) — in veggie capsules. 


    iTested Verified

    Specifications

    • First available: 11/2021
    • Shipping weight: 0.23 lb

      Shipping weight

      The Shipping Weight includes the product, protective packaging material and the actual shipping box. In addition, the Shipping Weight may be adjusted for the Dimensional Weight (e.g. length, width & height) of a package. It is important to note that certain types of products (e.g. glass containers, liquids, fragile, refrigerated or ice packed) will often require protective packaging material. As such, these products will reflect a higher Shipping Weight compared to the unprotected product.
    • Product code: LKN-02090
    • UPC: 898220020904
    • Package quantity: 90 count
    • Dimensions: 4.35 x 2.25 x 2.2 in, 0.23 lb

    • Same Formula, New Label. You may receive either package while we transition

    Suggested use

    Take 1 capsule daily, with food. Best when taken as directed by a qualified healthcare professional.

    Other ingredients

    Main Ingredients
    Thiamin (as Thiamine HCl), Riboflavin (as Riboflavin-5-Phosphate), Niacin (as Nicotinic Acid and Niacinamide), Vitamin B6 (as Pyridoxine HCl and Pyridoxal-5-Phosphate) Folate (as (6S)-5-Methyltetrahydrofolic Acid, Glucosamine Salt), Vitamin B12 (as Methylcobalamin), Biotin (as d-Biotin), Pantothenic Acid (as d-Calcium Pantothenate), Choline (as Choline Dihydrogen Citrate), Inositol

    Other Ingredients
    Modified Cellulose (Hypromellose Veggie Capsule), Dicalcium Phosphate, Ascorbyl Palmitate, Microcrystalline Cellulose, and Silicon Dioxide (Silica).

    Not manufactured with milk, eggs, fish, crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soy, sesame, or gluten. Produced in an FDA-registered, third-party audited, and cGMP-compliant facility that may process other products that contain these allergens or ingredients.

    Warnings

    Keep out of reach of children. Consult with a licensed physician, pharmacist, naturopath or other qualified healthcare professional prior to taking dietary supplements.

    Sealed for your protection. Do not use if seal is missing or broken. Store in a cool, dry place. Protect from heat, light, and moisture.

    While iHerb strives to ensure the accuracy of its product images and information, some manufacturing changes to packaging and/or ingredients may be pending update on our site. Although items may occasionally ship with alternate packaging, freshness is always guaranteed. We recommend that you read labels, warnings and directions of all products before use and not rely solely on the information provided by iHerb.

    Most relevantNewly answeredMost helpfulNewestOldest

    Does anybody developed allergic reaction from it? Because yesterday i got allergic reaction after taking it but I am not sure if it is the cause

    iHerb customer
    May 16, 2022
    Verified purchase
    Best answer
    Rewarded answer

    I also take this supplement & work in healthcare. Some people are sensitive to Niacin. Niacin can cause temporary flushing/redness/rash. This usually occurs within hours of taking it. This occurs for me on a mild level but dissipates quickly . It is essentially harmless. I suggest you check with your medical provider to determine if this was the cause. If this is the cause than a Niacin free supplement might be best for you.

    Disclaimer:Not medical or professional advice.
    See more answers (33)

    i have an 8 yr old daughter with myelomeningocele, current condition is can walk ,perform like normal kids but cannot control her bowel and urine so until now shes wearing diaper, and cannot balance herself qhen standing, can she take this vitamin? thank you for your time

    iHerb customer
    Aug 6, 2025
    Verified purchase
    Best answer

    A B complex or Nuttional Yeast should be ok. This supplement is better for adult's due to it having ACTIVE B3/ NICOTINIC ACID. This will cause a niacin flush and this will be distressing for both of you.

    Disclaimer:Not medical or professional advice.
